questions.question: The two main parts of a Java program are the class declaration and the ______ method.
If the class name is Main, the file name should be ______.
Every Java statement must end with a ______.
System.out.______() prints and then moves to the next line.
System.out.______() prints on the same line.
The data type used to store text is ______.
byte, short, and int are ______ data types.
The data type used for decimal numbers with more precision is ______.
A ______ is a named storage location.
Assigning a value to a variable is called ______.
The actual value written directly in a program is called a ______.
The operator ______ gives the remainder after division.
In the expression 100 + 800, 100 and 800 are called ______.
A ______ is a piece of code that produces a value when evaluated.
A binary operator needs ______ operands.
The simple assignment operator is ______.
In int age = 13;, age is the ______ name.
In int age = 13;, 13 is the ______.
The operator + performs ______.
The operator / performs ______.
The operator * performs ______.
The operator - performs ______.
The expression 10 + 5 gives the value ______.
The expression 17 % 5 gives the value ______.
The method where execution begins is the ______ method.
The keyword used to define a class is ______.
Curly brackets are used to define code ______.
The byte data type has the range ______ to 127.
The statement System.out.println("Hello"); ends with a ______.
The value on the right side of x = 25 is assigned to the variable on the ______ side.
